regions.label: paragraph_title
regions.text: CARD OF THANKS

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Robert T. Hall, 3233 Indiana avenue, waiter on Twentythield Indiana Limited, and many friends for their kindness while she underwent a painful operation. Also for the excellent services of Dr. S. Locke, an experienced name, and his assistant at Providence Hospital.

We wish to express our appreciation and thanks to our host of friends for their kindness during the illness and our friendship, especially for the beautiful floral offerings—Wilbur Perkus, husband; Mrs. Addie Wildkins, mother; Earl and Corine Wildins, brother and sister.

Chares C. Evans, father, sisters and mother, Miss, wish to thank the many friends who were so kind to him in his recent bereavement, and also extend sympathy to his wife.

Dear Friends: I take this means of the heartiness shown me and the comforting words during my sad bereavement of my only child, Mrs. Addie May Crawford, Miss, wish to thank the many friends who so lazily contributed the beautiful design. May God bless you all—Mrs. Mary E. Brown, mother, St. Paul, Minn.

I wish to thank my friends for the kindness and sympathy shown during my beloved wife, Patry, who died Dec. 31, 1920; also for the beautiful floral design—Samuel M. Lackland.

To those who were kind enough to respond to our appeal for the Christmas gift, we extend our sincere thanks. Committee, Jennie L. Aventrock, chairman, Jennie S. Aventrock, secretary; Mrs. S. Abel Jayes, Jes. Wickleffa and Jes. Haddox.

regions.label: text
regions.text:
In memory of Hortense Meaux Beinson, deceased Jan. 18, 1617. Beloved in your brothers, Edward R. Cromley, Compton, Annie Compton, clister, Grand Junction, Mich.

Richard Wilson, died Jan. 18, 1920.
"Dear Son, today recalls sad memories of your brother, rest; Those who think of you best.
Are the ones who love you best."—Loving mother and brother, ALICE E. WILSON and ROBERT RECORD.

Laurence N. Jones, died Jan. 12, 1920.
"Give my grief for one removed. They can be comforted. Trust I live in thee, and there I find him wiltier to be loved."—FLORENCE M. JONES, wife.

In loving memory of our father and husband, Toney Short, who died June 18, 1923.
"With loving-heart, dear father, We will try to follow you, He will love you, Heaven, all our pleasures to renew Rest, dear father; Jesus keep you, With a kind and loving hand, Crimson and light, Land, that bright and better land."—Loving daughter, Frank Howard, Godin.

In loving memory of our dear father, James Randle, Sr., who died one year ago, December 26, 1920.

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Oft and oft our thoughts do wander to
a grave so far away,

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Where they laid you, father darling,
Just one year ago."

regions.label: text
regions.text:
Loving and lonely children—Sarah R. Long, James Randle, Jr., Ozle Randle.

In sad and loving memory of my darling wife, Mary, who died one year ago, Jan. 21, 1920.

regions.label: text
regions.text: "She is sleeping in the cold, cold, ground;

regions.label: text
regions.text:
The dark blue skies are above her
She was too good and sweet for earth
None knew her but to love her;
Her fair form has faded now.

regions.label: text
regions.text:
So patient, tender and kind.
What beautiful memory you left behind.
—Loving husband, Robert Groves.

baby, 1. Robinson, who died
Jan. 21, 1920.

regions.text: Miss. Adèle Crawford Ridley was young Ohio, and she was this city when quite old. She attended Bethel and Quinn Chapel A M; and Sunday schools, was converted to a school under Miss Gertrude Jackson and proved a brilliant pupil. It was quite rare quality. Under the training of Miss Jackson she made her first apprentice to her to Chicago Medical College, receiving two certificates. She made her professional debut on a Bethel Church and then called the "Little Queen of Song." She her went to St. Paul and became the mother of four children—Robert and Clinton, twins; Leah May and her husband was an old sweetheart and school chum, Oscar Ridley, and they moved toingham, Ala. James A. Mundy helped her greatly in her musical career, and she the choir of one of the largest Baptist churches in Birmingham and was Steel Company male chorus. Alphene
